title: What is the cascade keyword in CSS?
description: How does the CSS cascade determine which styles apply?
options: The cascade resolves style conflicts using: 1) origin and importance, 2) specificity, 3) source order. Later rules override earlier ones at the same specificity
correct_answer: The cascade resolves style conflicts using: 1) origin and importance, 2) specificity, 3) source order. Later rules override earlier ones at the same specificity

title: What is the inherit keyword?
description: What does the inherit value do?
options: Forces a property to take the computed value from its parent element, even for non-inherited properties
correct_answer: Forces a property to take the computed value from its parent element, even for non-inherited properties

title: What is the initial keyword?
options: Resets a property to its initial value as defined in the CSS specification (not the browser default)
correct_answer: Resets a property to its initial value as defined in the CSS specification (not the browser default)

title: What is the unset keyword?
options: Acts as inherit for inherited properties and initial for non-inherited properties — effectively removes the author's declaration
correct_answer: Acts as inherit for inherited properties and initial for non-inherited properties — effectively removes the author's declaration

title: What is the revert keyword?
options: Rolls back the property value to the browser's default stylesheet (user-agent styles), unlike initial which uses the CSS spec default
correct_answer: Rolls back the property value to the browser's default stylesheet (user-agent styles), unlike initial which uses the CSS spec default

title: What is the difference between margin collapse and margin behavior?
options: Vertical margins of adjacent block-level elements collapse into a single margin (the larger one wins); doesn't happen with floats, flex/grid items, or inline-block
correct_answer: Vertical margins of adjacent block-level elements collapse into a single margin (the larger one wins); doesn't happen with floats, flex/grid items, or inline-block

title: What is BEM naming convention?
options: Block, Element, Modifier — a CSS naming methodology: .block__element--modifier; promotes reusable, maintainable component-based styles
correct_answer: Block, Element, Modifier — a CSS naming methodology: .block__element--modifier; promotes reusable, maintainable component-based styles

title: What is the difference between transform and position for animation?
description: Why prefer transform over top/left for animations?
options: transform (translate) is GPU-accelerated and doesn't trigger layout recalculation; top/left changes trigger layout+paint, causing jank
correct_answer: transform (translate) is GPU-accelerated and doesn't trigger layout recalculation; top/left changes trigger layout+paint, causing jank

title: What is the clip-path property?
options: Creates a clipping region that defines which part of an element is visible; supports shapes like circle(), ellipse(), polygon(), and path()
correct_answer: Creates a clipping region that defines which part of an element is visible; supports shapes like circle(), ellipse(), polygon(), and path()

title: What is the filter property?
options: blur(), brightness(), contrast(), grayscale(), hue-rotate(), invert(), opacity(), saturate(), sepia(), drop-shadow() — applied to the element and its contents
correct_answer: blur(), brightness(), contrast(), grayscale(), hue-rotate(), invert(), opacity(), saturate(), sepia(), drop-shadow() — applied to the element and its contents

title: What is the backdrop-filter property?
description: How does backdrop-filter differ from filter?
options: backdrop-filter applies effects to the area behind the element (not the element itself); used for glassmorphism/frosted glass effects
correct_answer: backdrop-filter applies effects to the area behind the element (not the element itself); used for glassmorphism/frosted glass effects

title: What is a CSS reset vs normalize?
description: How do CSS resets and normalize differ?
options: Reset strips all default styles to zero; Normalize preserves useful defaults while fixing cross-browser inconsistencies. Normalize is generally preferred
correct_answer: Reset strips all default styles to zero; Normalize preserves useful defaults while fixing cross-browser inconsistencies. Normalize is generally preferred

title: What is the pointer-events property?
options: Makes the element invisible to mouse/touch events; clicks pass through to elements behind it. Useful for overlays and disabled states
correct_answer: Makes the element invisible to mouse/touch events; clicks pass through to elements behind it. Useful for overlays and disabled states

title: What is the writing-mode property?
options: Controls whether text flows horizontally or vertically and sets the direction of block flow; values: horizontal-tb, vertical-rl, vertical-lr
correct_answer: Controls whether text flows horizontally or vertically and sets the direction of block flow; values: horizontal-tb, vertical-rl, vertical-lr

title: What is the user-select property?
options: Prevents the user from selecting text within the element; useful for UI elements that shouldn't be selectable
correct_answer: Prevents the user from selecting text within the element; useful for UI elements that shouldn't be selectable

title: What are CSS counters?
options: counter-reset, counter-increment, and counter() create automatic numbering in CSS without modifying HTML; useful for custom ordered lists and section numbering
correct_answer: counter-reset, counter-increment, and counter() create automatic numbering in CSS without modifying HTML; useful for custom ordered lists and section numbering

title: What is the @supports rule?
options: Feature detection in CSS: applies styles only if the browser supports a specified CSS property-value pair; e.g., @supports (display: grid) { }
correct_answer: Feature detection in CSS: applies styles only if the browser supports a specified CSS property-value pair; e.g., @supports (display: grid) { }

title: What is the currentColor keyword?
options: Refers to the element's computed color property value; useful for making borders, shadows, and SVG fills match the text color automatically
correct_answer: Refers to the element's computed color property value; useful for making borders, shadows, and SVG fills match the text color automatically

title: Build a CSS grid masonry layout.
description: Create a masonry-like layout using CSS Grid.

title: Build a responsive sidebar layout.
description: Create a layout with a fixed sidebar and flexible main content area.

title: Build a CSS-only accordion.
description: Create an accordion using only CSS with the details/summary elements.

title: What is the :nth-child selector?
options: Selects elements based on their position among siblings: :nth-child(2n) for evens, :nth-child(odd) for odds, :nth-child(3n+1) for every third starting from first
correct_answer: Selects elements based on their position among siblings: :nth-child(2n) for evens, :nth-child(odd) for odds, :nth-child(3n+1) for every third starting from first

title: What is the difference between :nth-child and :nth-of-type?
options: :nth-child counts all siblings regardless of type; :nth-of-type counts only siblings of the same element type
correct_answer: :nth-child counts all siblings regardless of type; :nth-of-type counts only siblings of the same element type

title: What is the word-break property?
options: Controls how words break at the end of a line: normal (default), break-all (break anywhere), keep-all (only break at allowed points for CJK)
correct_answer: Controls how words break at the end of a line: normal (default), break-all (break anywhere), keep-all (only break at allowed points for CJK)

title: What is the overscroll-behavior property?
options: Controls what happens when a scroll area is scrolled past its boundary: auto (default chain), contain (prevent chaining), none (prevent chaining + overscroll effects)
correct_answer: Controls what happens when a scroll area is scrolled past its boundary: auto (default chain), contain (prevent chaining), none (prevent chaining + overscroll effects)

title: What is the touch-action property?
options: Controls how touch gestures are handled by the browser: none (disable all), manipulation (only pan/zoom), pan-x, pan-y, pinch-zoom — important for custom touch interactions
correct_answer: Controls how touch gestures are handled by the browser: none (disable all), manipulation (only pan/zoom), pan-x, pan-y, pinch-zoom — important for custom touch interactions

title: Build a CSS gradient border.
description: Create an element with a gradient border effect.

title: What is the shape-outside property?
options: Defines a shape around which inline content wraps; works with floated elements to create non-rectangular text wrapping effects
correct_answer: Defines a shape around which inline content wraps; works with floated elements to create non-rectangular text wrapping effects

title: What is the difference between <b> and <strong>?
options: <strong> indicates important content (semantic); <b> is purely visual bold with no special meaning. Screen readers may emphasize <strong>
correct_answer: <strong> indicates important content (semantic); <b> is purely visual bold with no special meaning. Screen readers may emphasize <strong>

title: What is the role attribute?
description: What does the ARIA role attribute do?
options: Defines the purpose of an element for assistive technologies: role='navigation', role='button', role='dialog', etc. Prefer native HTML elements when possible
correct_answer: Defines the purpose of an element for assistive technologies: role='navigation', role='button', role='dialog', etc. Prefer native HTML elements when possible

title: What is the <meta viewport> tag?
description: Why is the viewport meta tag important?
options: Controls layout viewport behavior on mobile devices: width=device-width, initial-scale=1 ensures the page scales correctly on mobile screens
correct_answer: Controls layout viewport behavior on mobile devices: width=device-width, initial-scale=1 ensures the page scales correctly on mobile screens

title: What is the difference between SVG and Canvas?
description: When should you use SVG vs Canvas?
options: SVG: vector-based, DOM elements, scalable, good for icons/charts/interactive graphics. Canvas: pixel-based, immediate mode, better for complex animations/games/image processing
correct_answer: SVG: vector-based, DOM elements, scalable, good for icons/charts/interactive graphics. Canvas: pixel-based, immediate mode, better for complex animations/games/image processing

description: Create a CSS skeleton loading animation for a card layout.
